causalEstimates.lowPowerReason: p-value 0.202 exceeds 0.15; 95% confidence interval crosses zero
causalEstimates.interpretation: Estimated incremental effect for Meta Ads: $-2,077 (95% CI $-5,164 to $812); t=-1.27, p=0.202; parallel-trends check passed (0.1% pre-trend gap); observational difference-in-differences, low-power directional diagnostic only; not proof of incrementality.

causalEstimates.lowPowerReason: p-value 0.179 exceeds 0.15; 95% confidence interval crosses zero
causalEstimates.interpretation: Estimated incremental effect for Meta Ads: $2,238 (95% CI $-1,105 to $5,400); t=1.34, p=0.179; parallel-trends check passed (0.4% pre-trend gap); observational difference-in-differences, low-power directional diagnostic only; not proof of incrementality.

causalEstimates.interpretation: Estimated incremental effect for Google Ads: $-2,892 (95% CI $-6,306 to $1,192); t=-1.32, p=0.185; parallel-trends check passed (0.5% pre-trend gap); observational difference-in-differences, low-power directional diagnostic only; not proof of incrementality.

response_text:
{
  "executiveSummary": "Overall performance shows a slight decline with total revenue down -1.2% and average ROAS down -0.47%. Microsoft Ads continues to be the most efficient channel with a ROAS of 5.4407, while Meta Ads lags at 3.0003. Recent anomalies indicate a dip in Google Ads ROAS and Meta Ads revenue, suggesting potential efficiency challenges and creative fatigue. Strategic budget reallocation and focused testing are critical to reverse negative trends and capitalize on high-performing areas.",

response_json.executiveSummary: Overall performance shows a slight decline with total revenue down -1.2% and average ROAS down -0.47%. Microsoft Ads continues to be the most efficient channel with a ROAS of 5.4407, while Meta Ads lags at 3.0003. Recent anomalies indicate a dip in Google Ads ROAS and Meta Ads revenue, suggesting potential efficiency challenges and creative fatigue. Strategic budget reallocation and focused testing are critical to reverse negative trends and capitalize on high-performing areas.

revenueDrivers.title: Microsoft Ads efficiency
revenueDrivers.detail: Microsoft Ads is the strongest channel by ROAS, likely because its revenue per dollar is above the blended 4.06x benchmark; use the simulator to test whether that efficiency holds after incremental spend.

revenueDrivers.title: Forecast momentum
revenueDrivers.detail: The model projects $474,554 over the next 30 days because recent revenue trend is -1.2% while spend trend is -0.7%, indicating whether growth is volume-led or efficiency-led.

revenueDrivers.title: Measured spend association
revenueDrivers.detail: Google Ads has a strong positive spend-to-revenue association of 0.86 across 299 observations; this supports a budget test because association is evidence for a hypothesis, not proof of incrementality.

channelPerformance.insight: Revenue $2,329,653, spend $506,510, ROAS 4.60x; performance is consistent with spend quality and conversion rate jointly driving revenue.
channelPerformance.recommendation: Scale gradually because the causal risk is that higher spend worsens CPC or conversion quality; hold budget if intervals widen.

channelPerformance.insight: Revenue $1,223,308, spend $407,728, ROAS 3.00x; performance is consistent with spend quality and conversion rate jointly driving revenue.
channelPerformance.recommendation: Scale gradually because the causal risk is that higher spend worsens CPC or conversion quality; hold budget if intervals widen.

channelPerformance.insight: Revenue $617,636, spend $113,522, ROAS 5.44x; performance is consistent with spend quality and conversion rate jointly driving revenue.
channelPerformance.recommendation: Scale gradually because the causal risk is that higher spend worsens CPC or conversion quality; hold budget if intervals widen.

top.name: Shopping Best Sellers
top.insight: Generated $823,638 at 4.28x ROAS, likely due to stronger conversion quality relative to spend.

top.name: Brand Search
top.insight: Generated $774,144 at 5.37x ROAS, likely due to stronger conversion quality relative to spend.

top.name: Generic Search
top.insight: Generated $731,871 at 4.31x ROAS, likely due to stronger conversion quality relative to spend.

bottom.name: Cart Retargeting
bottom.issue: Low relative efficiency at 2.99x ROAS, consistent with spend not converting into revenue at the blended benchmark.
bottom.action: Review bids, audiences and creative because the causal failure point is likely click quality, conversion rate, or offer fit before adding budget.

bottom.name: Lookalike Prospecting
bottom.issue: Low relative efficiency at 3.00x ROAS, consistent with spend not converting into revenue at the blended benchmark.
bottom.action: Review bids, audiences and creative because the causal failure point is likely click quality, conversion rate, or offer fit before adding budget.

bottom.name: Advantage Shopping
bottom.issue: Low relative efficiency at 3.01x ROAS, consistent with spend not converting into revenue at the blended benchmark.
bottom.action: Review bids, audiences and creative because the causal failure point is likely click quality, conversion rate, or offer fit before adding budget.

budgetAllocation.rationale: ROAS is 4.60x versus blended 4.06x, so the likely causal test is whether incremental spend keeps conversion quality above the blended benchmark.
budgetAllocation.expectedImpact: Improve blended ROAS while protecting forecast revenue because spend shifts toward channels with stronger observed conversion economics.

budgetAllocation.rationale: ROAS is 3.00x versus blended 4.06x, so the likely causal test is whether incremental spend keeps conversion quality above the blended benchmark.
budgetAllocation.expectedImpact: Improve blended ROAS while protecting forecast revenue because spend shifts toward channels with stronger observed conversion economics.

budgetAllocation.rationale: ROAS is 5.44x versus blended 4.06x, so the likely causal test is whether incremental spend keeps conversion quality above the blended benchmark.
budgetAllocation.expectedImpact: Improve blended ROAS while protecting forecast revenue because spend shifts toward channels with stronger observed conversion economics.

risks.title: Forecast uncertainty
risks.description: Revenue intervals widen with longer horizons and budget changes because compounding spend, conversion-rate, and seasonality assumptions have more time to drift.
risks.mitigation: Treat 60 and 90-day views as planning ranges, not exact targets, because residual volatility and thinner segment history widen uncertainty as the horizon extends.

risks.title: Attribution dependency
risks.description: The model treats provided attribution as source of truth, so missing campaign or tracking rows can cause revenue and ROAS to be assigned to the wrong driver.
risks.mitigation: Monitor tracking gaps and campaign naming consistency before each forecast run.

risks.title: Spend efficiency drift
risks.description: Marginal ROAS may decline when spend is scaled too quickly because ROAS trend is -0.5% against spend trend -0.7%.
risks.mitigation: Increase budgets in staged increments and compare forecast vs actual weekly.

growthOpportunities.title: Scale Microsoft Ads
growthOpportunities.description: The strongest ROAS channel is the first candidate for controlled spend increases because it is above blended ROAS at 4.06x.
growthOpportunities.expectedImpact: Potential revenue lift with lower downside because incremental dollars start from the strongest observed efficiency base.

growthOpportunities.title: Repair underperformers
growthOpportunities.description: Meta Ads should be optimized before additional spend because weak ROAS points to conversion quality or CPC pressure rather than a budget shortage.
growthOpportunities.expectedImpact: ROAS recovery and lower wasted spend if the underlying click-to-revenue mechanism improves.

growthOpportunities.title: Use budget simulator weekly
growthOpportunities.description: Re-run 30, 60 and 90-day scenarios as campaign data refreshes because anomaly and trend-break signals can change the causal hypothesis behind each budget move.
growthOpportunities.expectedImpact: Better media planning discipline and faster risk detection.

actionPlan.action: Review budget simulator scenarios for Microsoft Ads and Meta Ads before the next media change.
actionPlan.kpi: Forecast revenue lift and blended ROAS

actionPlan.action: Audit campaign naming, attribution consistency and negative-spend/revenue anomalies before final submission.
Compare actual revenue against the 30-day forecast band and recalibrate if coverage drifts.
Shift incremental spend toward channels with above-average ROAS and stable forecast intervals.
actionPlan.kpi: Revenue growth with ROAS at or above target

causalHypotheses.title: Google Ads ROAS compression (May 16)
causalHypotheses.hypothesis: Google Ads revenue changed because a spend, demand, or campaign-mix shift produced an observational DiD effect of $-3,006.
supportingEvidence: DiD estimate for Google Ads: $-3,006 incremental revenue.
contradictingEvidence: No randomized incrementality test is available; attribution remains observational.
causalHypotheses.recommendedTest: Run a controlled budget holdout or staged budget ramp and compare actual revenue against the forecast interval.

causalHypotheses.title: Google Ads ROAS compression (Jun 11)
causalHypotheses.hypothesis: Google Ads revenue changed because a spend, demand, or campaign-mix shift produced an observational DiD effect of $-2,892.
supportingEvidence: DiD estimate for Google Ads: $-2,892 incremental revenue.
contradictingEvidence: No randomized incrementality test is available; attribution remains observational.
causalHypotheses.recommendedTest: Run a controlled budget holdout or staged budget ramp and compare actual revenue against the forecast interval.

causalHypotheses.title: Meta Ads ROAS lift (Jun 08)
causalHypotheses.hypothesis: Meta Ads revenue changed because a spend, demand, or campaign-mix shift produced an observational DiD effect of $2,238.
supportingEvidence: DiD estimate for Meta Ads: $2,238 incremental revenue.
contradictingEvidence: No randomized incrementality test is available; attribution remains observational.
causalHypotheses.recommendedTest: Run a controlled budget holdout or staged budget ramp and compare actual revenue against the forecast interval.

causalHypotheses.title: Google Ads spend-efficiency relationship
causalHypotheses.hypothesis: Google Ads may be driving incremental revenue because spend deltas and revenue deltas move together with correlation 0.86.
supportingEvidence: Spend/revenue delta correlation for Google Ads: 0.86.
contradictingEvidence: Correlation evidence cannot separate channel causality from seasonality, promotions, or tracking mix.
causalHypotheses.recommendedTest: Scale the channel in staged increments and monitor marginal ROAS versus the forecast baseline.

causalHypotheses.title: Google Ads anomaly signal (Jun 11)
causalHypotheses.hypothesis: Google Ads performance may have shifted because an anomaly or trend break changed the recent baseline used for forecasting.
supportingEvidence: Detected anomaly count=46 and trend-break count=42.
contradictingEvidence: A single anomaly may be a tracking or reporting artifact unless it repeats across adjacent days.
causalHypotheses.recommendedTest: Audit tracking and campaign changes around the signal date, then rerun the forecast after excluding confirmed data errors.

contradictingEvidence: Observational DiD is not a randomized lift test.
Other channels or promotion timing may still explain part of the movement.
llmHypothesisRanking.recommendedValidation: Run a staged budget holdout or geo split around the affected channel.
llmHypothesisRanking.rationale: The strongest statistical candidate is Google Ads because it has the largest ranked DiD/anomaly evidence among supplied signals.

contradictingEvidence: A single-day anomaly can be a tracking or data latency artifact.
The same pattern could be explained by seasonality or campaign edits.
llmHypothesisRanking.recommendedValidation: Audit platform change history, bids, budgets, and tracking around the anomaly window.
llmHypothesisRanking.rationale: A sharp break is consistent with an external platform or delivery change, but needs operational evidence.
